evidence_basis_summary: C015_S1 shows humans adjudicate relevance by manually reranking AI ideas and verifying human ideas, and defining 7 NLP topics from conference CfPs; C015_P1 relies on algorithmic filtering of papers by Faiss with no human relevance gate.
notes: Upgraded from low to medium based on direct first-author manual rerank in C015_S1 and dataset filtering choices in C015_P1.

evidence_basis_summary: Novelty/feasibility scoring frameworks and idea templates in C015_S1 and the self-assessment and novelty-voting steps in C015_P1 impose mechanism constraints, though neither source targets social-science causal interpretation.
notes: Upgraded from low to medium; sources imply role indirectly via scoring rubrics and idea templates.

evidence_basis_summary: Both sources operate in CS/NLP benchmark settings with fixed topic taxonomies (seven NLP topics in C015_S1; CS dataset in C015_P1), so construct and perspective boundary setting in the social-science sense is out of scope.
notes: Upgraded from low to medium: text confirms not-applicable because domain is CS/NLP ideation, not social-science construct work.

evidence_basis_summary: Corpus is AMiner CS / OAG 3.1 papers in C015_P1 and Semantic Scholar retrieval via RAG in C015_S1; no pluralism over social-science evidence bases is attempted or needed.
notes: Upgraded from low to medium; sources directly describe corpus construction and confirm role is not applicable to social-science agenda work.

evidence_basis_summary: C015_P1 specifies a five-step pipeline with prompts and metric definitions; C015_S1 details IRB approval, recruitment screening, style-normalization, review form design, and Swiss-tournament ranking as explicit human-designed protocol.
notes: Upgraded from low to high and status changed from not_applicable to robust based on extensive protocol/provenance work in both sources; observable changed to yes.

evidence_basis_summary: The case is framed as a transportability pressure test; C015_S1 explicitly limits claims to prompting-NLP and flags self-evaluation failures and diversity gaps, and C015_P1 acknowledges CS-only training data and bound leakage caveats.
notes: Upgraded from low to high and changed to observable yes/conditional: both papers directly discuss the limits of transporting idea-generation claims beyond their tested domains.

evidence_basis_summary: Neither source uses fieldwork, interviews, or contextual ethnographic interpretation; C015_P1 runs fully virtual agents and C015_S1 uses blind asynchronous review.
notes: Upgraded from low to high; text directly confirms role is not applicable.

evidence_basis_summary: C015_S1 assigns authorship to the three Stanford authors, pays participants, runs IRB, and has the first author do manual rerank and verification; C015_P1 lists a large author team and masks real scientist names to manage privacy responsibility.
notes: Upgraded from low to high and status from unclear to transformed; role is clearly visible as human authors retain accountability while LLM agents execute generation.

evidence_basis_summary: Topic taxonomies are prespecified from conference CfPs (C015_S1) or dataset metadata (C015_P1); no social-science category-definition work is performed.
notes: Upgraded from low to medium; not-applicable confirmed because construct work is absent from both sources.

evidence_basis_summary: C015_S1 aggregates reviewer scores and C015_P1 uses majority voting among agents; subgroup disagreement is collapsed into averages rather than preserved as pluralism.
notes: Upgraded from low to medium; sources directly describe aggregation that forecloses perspective sampling.

evidence_basis_summary: Both sources pressure whether ideation results transport: C015_S1 restricts claims to prompting-NLP and calls out open problems; C015_P1 notes CS-domain specificity and data-leakage caveats.
notes: Upgraded from low to high and to observable yes/conditional; this split-candidate version of the role is the most directly evidenced in the case.

evidence_basis_summary: C015_P1 calibrates the ON novelty metric to human expert ratings (Pearson 0.52) and benchmarks against HypoGen and AI Scientist; C015_S1 calibrates the LLM ranker against 1.2K ICLR submissions with review scores.
notes: Upgraded from low to high and to observable yes/conditional; calibration architecture is explicit in both sources.

evidence_basis_summary: No interview or field setting exists in either source; C015_S1 uses anonymized asynchronous reviewing and C015_P1 uses virtual agents only.
notes: Upgraded from low to high; not-applicable directly confirmed.

evidence_basis_summary: No archival or tacit contextual interpretation is required; both sources work with machine-retrievable paper metadata and standardized idea templates.
notes: Upgraded from low to high; not-applicable directly confirmed.

evidence_basis_summary: C015_S1 uses LLM-based paper retrieval and Swiss-tournament ranking to triage 4000 seed ideas down to candidates; C015_P1 uses Faiss retrieval and agent voting to screen ideas, with humans auditing rather than screening first-pass.
notes: Upgraded from low to high and changed to observable yes/shrinking: first-pass screening is clearly delegated to LLMs, with human rerank as exception.

role_label: Routine coder on high-consensus, ground-truth-rich tasks
evidence_basis_summary: Neither source involves high-consensus ground-truth coding; C015_S1 reports novelty judgments are subjective even among experts and C015_P1 uses proxy metrics rather than gold labels.
notes: Upgraded from low to medium; sources imply role is not applicable because ideation lacks ground-truth labels.

evidence_basis_summary: C015_S1 uses an LLM style-normalization module to rewrite both human and AI idea proposals into a uniform style, and C015_P1 has agents iteratively revise abstracts across K turns; prose drafting is explicitly delegated to LLMs.
notes: Upgraded from low to high and to observable yes/shrinking: style normalization and iterative abstract refinement are directly described as LLM tasks.
